---
title: "master_analysis_usgs"
format: pdf
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r, load packages and working directory}

# data import and database management 
# reading files, reshaping data, and efficient data handling 

#######################################################

library(readxl) # import excel spreadsheets
library(data.table) #quick manipulation of large datasets
library(stringr) # text and string processing 
library(lubridate) # data and time handling 
library(writexl) # exports editted databases into excel files

#######################################################

# General data science workflow 
# Collection of packages for data manipulation and plotting

#######################################################

library(tidyverse)

#######################################################

#Statistical analyses and mixed models 
# Hypothesis testing, summaries, and model fitting 

#######################################################

library(gt) # displaying formatted tables for statistics 
library(rstatix) # statistical tests and assumption checking
library(lmerTest) # p-values and tests for lme4 models 
library(lme4) # linear and generalized mixed-effects models
library(ggeffects) # predicted values and marginal effects 

#######################################################

# Data visualization 
# Plot creation, figure assembly, and publication formatting 

#######################################################

library(patchwork) #comvine ggplots into multi-panel figures
library(cowplot) # figure arrangement and plot annotations
library(rcartocolor) # color palettes
library(ggh4x) # advanced ggplot extensions
library(scales) # axis labels, formatting, and scaling 

#######################################################
# Spatial data and mapping 
# GIS data handling and map visualization
#######################################################

library(sf) #spatial vector data
library(ggmap) #basemaps and geographic visualization
library(ggspatial) #spatial annotations and map elements
library(leaflet) #interactive web maps

#######################################################

#Tables and report outputs 
# Interactive tables and report presentation 

#######################################################

library(reactable) #interactive tables for reports

#######################################################
# R markdown and document rendering 
# PDF generation and LaTeX support 
#######################################################

library(tinytex) #LaTeX engine for PDF knitting



```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r, working directory and consistent plot theme}

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = 'C:/Users/savan/OneDrive - San Francisco State University/ProjectData/GitHub/DepositionSFB/data')
```

```{r, load raw data and prep}

sed_usgs <- read.csv("C:/Users/savan/OneDrive - San Francisco State University/ProjectData/GitHub/DepositionSFB/data/usgs_SFB_sediment_pad_data.csv")

str(sed_usgs)


# Clean whitespace from character columns only
sed_usgs$Dist <- str_replace_all(sed_usgs$Dist, " ", "")
sed_usgs$Tran <- str_replace_all(sed_usgs$Tran, " ", "")
sed_usgs$Year     <- str_replace_all(sed_usgs$Year, " ", "")
sed_usgs$Site    <- str_replace_all(sed_usgs$Site, " ", "")
sed_usgs$Replicate    <- str_replace_all(sed_usgs$Replicate, " ", "")
sed_usgs$Flux   <- str_replace_all(sed_usgs$Flux, " ", "")


# Change to factor (independent variables) and numeric (dependent variables)

sed_usgs$Dist = as.character(sed_usgs$Dist)
sed_usgs$Date.placed.in.field = as.Date(sed_usgs$Date.placed.in.field, 
                                          format = "%m/%d/%Y"   # need this so R doesn't automatically assume format
)
sed_usgs$Date.removed.from.field = as.Date(sed_usgs$Date.removed.from.field,            format = "%m/%d/%Y"   # need this so R doesn't automatically assume format
)

sed_usgs$Site <- as.factor(sed_usgs$Site)
sed_usgs$Tran <- as.factor(sed_usgs$Tran)
sed_usgs$Dist <- as.factor(sed_usgs$Dist)
sed_usgs$Replicate<- as.factor(sed_usgs$Replicate)
sed_usgs$Year<- as.factor(sed_usgs$Year)
sed_usgs$Flux <- as.numeric(sed_usgs$Flux)


# Making a Marsh Edge Type Categorization 

sed_usgs <- sed_usgs %>% 
  mutate(marsh_edge_type = case_when(
    Site %in% c("CorteMadera") ~ "Scarped", 
    Site %in% c("SanPablo") ~ "Ramped", 
    TRUE ~ "Other"
  ), 
  marsh_edge_type = factor(marsh_edge_type, levels = c("Scarped", "Ramped"))
  )

#Making a Marsh Orientation Categorization

sed_usgs <- sed_usgs %>% 
  mutate(marsh_orientation = case_when(
    Site %in% c("CorteMadera") ~ "East.facing", 
    Site %in% c("SanPablo") ~ "South.facing", 
    TRUE ~ "Other"
  ), 
  marsh_orientation = factor(marsh_orientation, levels = c("East.facing", "South.facing", "West.facing"))
  )


# Adding a Marsh Area category

sed_usgs <- sed_usgs %>% 
  mutate(Marsh_Area = case_when (
    Dist %in% c("0.5", "2", "6") ~ "Edge", 
    Dist %in% c("12", "18", "24") ~ "Platform", 
    Dist %in% c("48") ~ "Upland"
  ), 
  Marsh_Area = factor(Marsh_Area, levels = c("Edge", "Platform", "Upland")) 
  )




#If your dates look like "01/15/2023" (MM/DD/YYYY)
#sed_usgs <- sed_usgs %>%
#mutate(Date.placed.in.field = as.Date(Date.placed.in.field, format = "%m/%d/%Y"))
#mutate(Date.removed.from.field = as.Date(Date.removed.from.field, format = "%m/%d/%Y"))


sed_usgs <- sed_usgs %>% 
  mutate(Study.Phase = case_when(
    Site == "CorteMadera" & Date.placed.in.field >= as.Date("6/29/2022", format = "%m/%d/%Y") & Date.removed.from.field <= as.Date("7/27/2022", format = "%m/%d/%Y") ~ "M1",
    Site == "CorteMadera" & Date.placed.in.field >= as.Date("7/27/2022", format = "%m/%d/%Y") & Date.removed.from.field <= as.Date("8/24/2022", format = "%m/%d/%Y") ~ "M2",
    Site == "CorteMadera" & Date.placed.in.field >= as.Date("8/24/2022", format = "%m/%d/%Y") & Date.removed.from.field <= as.Date("9/21/2022", format = "%m/%d/%Y") ~ "M3",
    Site == "CorteMadera" & Date.placed.in.field >= as.Date("9/21/2022", format = "%m/%d/%Y") & Date.removed.from.field <= as.Date("10/25/2022", format = "%m/%d/%Y") ~ "M4",
    Site == "CorteMadera" & Date.placed.in.field >= as.Date("10/25/2022", format = "%m/%d/%Y") & Date.removed.from.field <= as.Date("11/30/2022", format = "%m/%d/%Y") ~ "M5",
    Site == "CorteMadera" & Date.placed.in.field >= as.Date("11/30/2022", format = "%m/%d/%Y") & Date.removed.from.field <= as.Date("12/21/2022", format = "%m/%d/%Y") ~ "M6",
    Site == "CorteMadera" & Date.placed.in.field >= as.Date("12/21/2022", format = "%m/%d/%Y") & Date.removed.from.field <= as.Date("1/18/2023", format = "%m/%d/%Y") ~ "M7",
    Site == "CorteMadera" & Date.placed.in.field >= as.Date("1/18/2023", format = "%m/%d/%Y") & Date.removed.from.field <= as.Date("2/17/2023", format = "%m/%d/%Y") ~ "M8",
    Site == "CorteMadera" & Date.placed.in.field >= as.Date("2/17/2023", format = "%m/%d/%Y") & Date.removed.from.field <= as.Date("3/23/2023", format = "%m/%d/%Y") ~ "M9",
    Site == "CorteMadera" & Date.placed.in.field >= as.Date("3/23/2023", format = "%m/%d/%Y") & Date.removed.from.field <= as.Date("4/20/2023", format = "%m/%d/%Y") ~ "M10",
    Site == "CorteMadera" & Date.placed.in.field >= as.Date("4/20/2023", format = "%m/%d/%Y") & Date.removed.from.field <= as.Date("5/18/2023", format = "%m/%d/%Y") ~ "M11",
    Site == "CorteMadera" & Date.placed.in.field >= as.Date("5/18/2023", format = "%m/%d/%Y") & Date.removed.from.field <= as.Date("6/20/2023", format = "%m/%d/%Y") ~ "M12",
    # San Pablo
    Site == "SanPablo" & Date.placed.in.field >= as.Date("6/30/2022", format = "%m/%d/%Y") & Date.removed.from.field <= as.Date("7/28/2022", format = "%m/%d/%Y") ~ "M1",
    Site == "SanPablo" & Date.placed.in.field >= as.Date("7/28/2022", format = "%m/%d/%Y") & Date.removed.from.field <= as.Date("8/25/2022", format = "%m/%d/%Y") ~ "M2",
    Site == "SanPablo" & Date.placed.in.field >= as.Date("8/25/2022", format = "%m/%d/%Y") & Date.removed.from.field <= as.Date("9/22/2022", format = "%m/%d/%Y") ~ "M3",
    Site == "SanPablo" & Date.placed.in.field >= as.Date("9/22/2022", format = "%m/%d/%Y") & Date.removed.from.field <= as.Date("10/26/2022", format = "%m/%d/%Y") ~ "M4",
    Site == "SanPablo" & Date.placed.in.field >= as.Date("10/26/2022", format = "%m/%d/%Y") & Date.removed.from.field <= as.Date("11/22/2022", format = "%m/%d/%Y") ~ "M5",
    Site == "SanPablo" & Date.placed.in.field >= as.Date("11/22/2022", format = "%m/%d/%Y") & Date.removed.from.field <= as.Date("12/20/2022", format = "%m/%d/%Y") ~ "M6",
    Site == "SanPablo" & Date.placed.in.field >= as.Date("12/20/2022", format = "%m/%d/%Y") & Date.removed.from.field <= as.Date("1/17/2023", format = "%m/%d/%Y") ~ "M7",
    Site == "SanPablo" & Date.placed.in.field >= as.Date("1/17/2023", format = "%m/%d/%Y") & Date.removed.from.field <= as.Date("2/15/2023", format = "%m/%d/%Y") ~ "M8",
    Site == "SanPablo" & Date.placed.in.field >= as.Date("2/15/2023", format = "%m/%d/%Y") & Date.removed.from.field <= as.Date("3/23/2023", format = "%m/%d/%Y") ~ "M9",
    Site == "SanPablo" & Date.placed.in.field >= as.Date("3/23/2023", format = "%m/%d/%Y") & Date.removed.from.field <= as.Date("4/21/2023", format = "%m/%d/%Y") ~ "M10",
    Site == "SanPablo" & Date.placed.in.field >= as.Date("4/21/2023", format = "%m/%d/%Y") & Date.removed.from.field <= as.Date("5/19/2023", format = "%m/%d/%Y") ~ "M11",
    Site == "SanPablo" & Date.placed.in.field >= as.Date("5/19/2023", format = "%m/%d/%Y") & Date.removed.from.field <= as.Date("6/21/2023", format = "%m/%d/%Y") ~ "M12",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  ))

sed_usgs$Study.Phase <- str_replace_all(sed_usgs$Study.Phase, " ", "")
sed_usgs$Study.Phase <- factor(sed_usgs$Study.Phase, levels = paste0("M", 1:12))
# Check replication to prevent design problems


#sed_usgs %>% count(Flux, Year) 
#sed_usgs %>% count(Year, Study.Phase)
#sed_usgs %>% count(Tran, Replicate)

master_usgs <- sed_usgs


str(master_usgs)
table(master_usgs$Site)
table(master_usgs$marsh_edge_type)
table(master_usgs$marsh_orientation)
table(master_usgs$Study.Phase)


readr::write_csv(master_usgs, "C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\data\\master_usgs.csv"
) 

str(master_usgs)



```

```{r}
sed_basic_usgs= ggplot(master_usgs, aes(x = Flux)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = expression(Flux~(g/m^3)), y = "Count") 

sed_basic_usgs
# Deposition by Edge Type 
sed_edge_usgs = ggplot(master_usgs, aes(x = marsh_edge_type, y = Flux)) +
  geom_boxplot() + 
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Marsh Edge Type", y = expression(Flux~(g/m^3))) 
sed_edge_usgs
# Deposition by Elevation (do this later once you've figured out z*) 
#sed_z= ggplot(master_usgs, aes(x = z*, y = Flux, color = marsh_edge_type)) +
#geom_boxplot() + 
#geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.5) +
# theme_minimal() +
#labs(x = "z*", y = expression(Flux~(g/m^3))) 
#sed_z

# Deposition by Distance from Marsh Edge 
sed_dist_edge_usgs= ggplot(master_usgs, aes(x = Dist, y = Flux, color = marsh_edge_type)) +
  geom_boxplot() + 
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Distance from the Marsh Edge (m) ", y = expression(Flux~(g/m^3))) 
sed_dist_edge_usgs
# Deposition by Hydrological Time

# Deposition by Season
sed_season_usgs= ggplot(master_usgs, aes(x = Season, y = Flux)) +
  geom_boxplot() + 
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Season ", y = expression(Flux~(g/m^3))) 
sed_season_usgs

# Deposition by Marsh Area
sed_edge_area_usgs= ggplot(master_usgs, aes(x = Marsh_Area, y = Flux, color = marsh_edge_type)) +
  geom_boxplot() + 
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Marsh Area ", y = expression(Flux~(g/m^3))) 
sed_edge_area_usgs

# Deposition by Season by Site
sed_site_season_usgs= ggplot(master_usgs, aes(x = Season, y = Flux, color = Site)) +
  geom_boxplot() + 
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Season ", y = expression(Flux~(g/m^3))) 
sed_site_season_usgs

# Deposition by Marsh Area by Site
sed_site_area_usgs= ggplot(master_usgs, aes(x = Marsh_Area, y = Flux, color = Site)) +
  geom_boxplot() + 
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Marsh Area ", y = expression(Flux~(g/m^3))) 
sed_site_area_usgs


# Deposition by Study Phase by Site
sed_phase_site_usgs = master_usgs %>%
  filter(!is.na(Study.Phase)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Study.Phase, y = Flux, color = Site)) +
  geom_boxplot() + 
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Study Phase ", y = expression(Flux~(g/m^3))) 
sed_phase_site_usgs

# Deposition by Study Phase by Site (log)
sed_phase_site_log_usgs = master_usgs %>%
  filter(!is.na(Study.Phase)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Study.Phase, y = Flux, color = Site)) +
  geom_boxplot() + 
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Study Phase ", y = expression(Flux~(g/m^3))) 
sed_phase_site_log_usgs

# Deposition by Study Phase by Marsh Edge Type

sed_phase_edge_usgs = master_usgs %>%
  filter(!is.na(Study.Phase)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Study.Phase, y = Flux, color = marsh_edge_type)) +
  geom_boxplot() + 
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Marsh Edge Type ", y = expression(Flux~(g/m^3))) 
sed_phase_edge_usgs

# Deposition by Study Phase Across Marsh Area
sed_phase_area_usgs = master_usgs %>%
  filter(!is.na(Study.Phase)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Marsh_Area, y = Flux, color = Study.Phase)) +
  facet_wrap(~Site) +
  geom_boxplot() + 
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Marsh Area ", y = expression(Flux~(g/m^3))
  ) 

sed_phase_area_usgs
```

```{r}

ggplot(
  master_usgs,
  aes(
    Dist,
    Flux
  )
) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  facet_wrap(~ Site, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_minimal()

usgs_dist_summary <- master_usgs %>%
  group_by(Site, Dist) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_flux = mean(Flux, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_flux = sd(Flux, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ggplot(
  usgs_dist_summary,
  aes(
    x = Dist,
    y = mean_flux
  )
) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = mean_flux - se_flux,
      ymax = mean_flux + se_flux
    ),
    width = 0.2
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ Site, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Distance from Marsh Edge (m)",
    y = expression(Mean~Flux~(g/m^3))
  )


usgs_phase_summary <- master_usgs %>%
  group_by(Study.Phase) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_flux = mean(Flux, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_flux = sd(Flux, na.rm = TRUE)/sqrt(n())
  )

ggplot(usgs_phase_summary,
       aes(Study.Phase, mean_flux)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = mean_flux - se_flux,
      ymax = mean_flux + se_flux
    ),
    width = 0.2
  )

```

```{r}


# flux through time 

phase_summary_usgs <- master_usgs %>%
  filter(Site == "CorteMadera") %>%
  group_by(Study.Phase) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_flux = mean(Flux, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_flux = sd(Flux, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

meanflux_time_c_usgs = ggplot(
  phase_summary,
  aes(x = Study.Phase, y = mean_flux, group = 1)
) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = mean_flux - se_flux,
      ymax = mean_flux + se_flux
    ),
    width = 0.2
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0,30)) +
  labs(
    title = paste("Mean Flux Through Time at", "Corte Madera"),
    subtitle = "Negative flux values set to zero",
    x = "Study Phase",
    y = expression(Mean~Flux~(g/m^3))
  )
meanflux_time_c_usgs

# Save the high-res image
ggsave(("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\meanflux_time_c_usgs.png"),
       plot = meanflux_time_c_usgs,
       width = 10,  
       height = 6,
       dpi = 300,
       bg = "white"
)
```

```{r, more detailed plots for time and space}

# mean flux per distance (study phase-time)

usgstime_dist_summary <- master_usgs %>%
  group_by(Dist, Study.Phase) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_flux = mean(Flux, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_flux = sd(Flux, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

usgsmeanflux_dist_sp_c = ggplot(
  usgstime_dist_summary,
  aes(x = Dist, y = mean_flux, group = 1)
) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = mean_flux - se_flux,
      ymax = mean_flux + se_flux
    ),
    width = 0.2
  ) +
  facet_wrap(vars(Study.Phase), scales = "fixed") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0,9.5)) +
  labs(
    title = paste("Mean Flux by Distance from Marsh Edge at", "Corte Madera Per Study Phase"),
    x = "Distance from Marsh Edge (m)",
    y = expression(Mean~Flux~(g/m^3))
  )

usgsmeanflux_dist_sp_c


# Save the high-res image
ggsave(("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\usgsmeanflux_dist_sp_c.png"),
       plot = meanflux_dist_sp_c,
       width = 10,  
       height = 6,
       dpi = 300,
       bg = "white"
)

#mean flux per study phase (distance)

usgsmeanflux_sp_dist_c = ggplot(
  usgstime_dist_summary,
  aes(x = Study.Phase, y = mean_flux, group = 1)
) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = mean_flux - se_flux,
      ymax = mean_flux + se_flux
    ),
    width = 0.2
  ) +
  facet_wrap(vars(Dist), scales = "fixed") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  labs(
    title = paste("Mean Flux by Distance from Marsh Edge at", "Corte Madera"),
    x = "Distance from Marsh Edge (m)",
    y = expression(Mean~Flux~(g/m^3))
  )



usgsmeanflux_sp_dist_c

# Save the high-res image
ggsave(("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\usgsmeanflux_sp_dist_c.png"),
       plot = meanflux_dist_c,
       width = 10,  
       height = 6,
       dpi = 300,
       bg = "white"
)
```
